1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	# Clean-room task: theory PRETTY-PRINTER
2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `pretty-clean` in `workspace/` that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the tamarin-prover binary's **theory echo** — the
5	`theory <name> begin … end` text it prints at theory-load. Given a
6	parsed/closed theory (data model in `interface/ast_types.rs`), produce
7	byte-identical render text and the API in `interface/required_api.md`.
8	
9	## The observable boundary
10	
11	`oracle/pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` runs the Haskell binary **WITHOUT
12	`--prove`** and prints the EXTRACTED THEORY ECHO — exactly the bytes you must
13	reproduce. Running without `--prove` is deliberate and load-bearing:
14	
15	* the echo prints at theory-load (~0.25 s/file), so the gate is fast; and
16	* **no proof search runs**, so every lemma renders `by sorry` and NO proof
17	  tree is emitted. The proof/constraint-system renderer is solver output, not
18	  pretty-printer surface, and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (see "Stays ported").
19	
20	The extracted block is `theory <name> begin … end` MINUS two trailing
21	formal-comment blocks the tool appends inside that span and everything after
22	`end`:
23	
24	* the wellformedness report (`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`
25	  or the multi-line `/*\nWARNING …\n*/`) — a **separate slice** owned by the
26	  wellformedness cluster; do not reimplement it here;
27	* the volatile `/*\nGenerated from: …\n*/` build stamp (version / git rev /
28	  compile time); and
29	* the `====… summary of summaries …` after `end` (carries processing time).
30	
31	Interior comments — per-rule `variants (modulo AC)` blocks, `has exactly the
32	trivial AC variant`, and `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples`
33	— are KEPT: they are your output. The extraction awk is embedded in the oracle
34	and in the acceptance gate identically, so
35	`diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(pretty-clean-render f)` == the gate verdict.
36	
37	## Acceptance gate (full corpus, primary oracle AND integration check)
38	
39	`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/pretty_gate.sh` runs the binary at `RS_PATH`
40	(point it at your build) no-prove over the whole 419-file corpus, extracts the
41	theory echo, and diffs it against a no-prove Haskell reference cache
42	(`scripts/.hs_pretty_cache`, auto-filled on first run). It reuses the batch
43	gate's `ckey`/`flags`/`strip_env` machinery so per-file canonical flags stay
44	identical. Output TSV: `relpath  MATCH|DIFF|SKIP_*  diffcount`. Warm-cache full
45	corpus ≈ 72 s at JOBS=6. Run it every iteration; `ALLOWLIST` subsets it while
46	you work a single sub-target. **Acceptance is the FULL-corpus gate green, not a
47	fixture subset** (the wf-regression lesson).
48	
49	Measured baseline of the CURRENT ported tree against this gate: **403 MATCH /
50	16 DIFF / 0 SKIP**. All 16 DIFFs are the single `features/auto-sources/spore/*`
51	family and are NOT pretty-printer divergences (see "Stays ported"). The
52	pure-render surface is at 100% today, so your target is: keep every one of the
53	403 green while the ported renderer is deleted underneath you.
54	
55	## Sub-target decomposition (each independently gate-checkable via ALLOWLIST)
56	
57	Ranked by (individual-author yield ÷ isolation) — a good sealed round takes ONE
58	cleanly-observable sub-component at a time. Corpus counts are over the 419-file
59	parity corpus.
60	
61	| # | sub-target | exercised by | ported LOC | isolation | authors it dents |
62	|---|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------------|
63	| **R1** | **term core + signature block** — `Term`→text (operators `^ * ⊕ ++ %+`, pairs `<..>`, `exp`, `diff`, sorts `~ $ # %`, constants `'x'`, `.idx`) AND the `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:` block | builtins 319, functions 229, equations 137; `^`251 `*`(mult) `⊕`38 `++`/mset 89 nat 2 | term `crates/tamarin-term/src/pretty.rs` ~410; sig fns in `pretty_theory.rs` (`render_signature`/`render_fun_syms`/`render_equations`) ~130 | HIGHEST — term is a pure leaf (`Term`→`String`, no solver), the foundation every other sub-target reuses; signature is the first block, minimal wrapping | term.rs: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rsasse, charlie-j, BTom-GH, rkunnema |
64	| **R2** | **rule rendering** — facts + premises/actions/conclusions `[..] --[..]-> [..]`, rule attributes, `variants (modulo AC)` text (substitutions supplied), loop-breakers | rules 332 | `render_rule`/`render_rule_body`/`render_ac_variants_block`/`rule_attributes_doc` in `pretty_theory.rs` ~600 | MODERATE — reuses R1 term/fact; variant SUBSTITUTIONS come pre-computed from the solver, you render them | bulk of `pretty_theory.rs` (25-author header) |
65	| **R3** | **restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering** — `∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊤ ⊥ @ < = ⊏ last(..)`, quantifier binder allocation, temporal vars `#i`, the guarded-formula comment | lemmas 384, restrictions 179, exists-trace 296 | `pretty_formula.rs` ~2800 (formula + guarded); lemma/restriction wrappers in `pretty_theory.rs` ~300 | MODERATE — reuses R1 for atom term args; formula layout is its own file | `pretty_formula.rs`: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rkunnema, rsasse, Hong-Thai, BTom-GH, charlie-j, arcz |
66	| **R4** | **macros / predicates blocks** | macros 9, predicates 12 | `render_parsed_macros`/`render_predicate` ~120 | VERY HIGH — tiny, self-contained | mop-up; no unique authors |
67	| — | **proof / heuristic / constraint-system annotations** | (only under `--prove` / web) | `pretty_system.rs` 702, proof fns in `pretty_theory.rs`, `pretty_hpj` proof paths | — | **STAYS PORTED** (solver-entangled — see below) |
68	
69	Recommended order: **R1 → R2 → R3 → R4**. R1 unblocks R2 and R3 (both consume
70	term rendering). Do R1 first: it is the deepest, most-reused, most-isolated
71	core, and it is what makes `pretty_theory.rs` (the 25-author file carrying the
72	pseudonymous web authors) deletable once R2/R3 also land.
73	
74	## Layout engine (NOT part of the erasure surface)
75	
76	Every renderer builds a HughesPJ `Doc` and renders it at width 110 / ribbon 73.
77	That layout algebra is BSD-licensed (Haskell `pretty-1.1.3.6`) and has ALREADY
78	been clean-roomed by the graphdot cluster
79	(`../graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs`, from
80	`../graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6`). REUSE it — do not re-derive the
81	fitting logic from the GPL side. Your job is the tamarin-specific part: WHICH
82	combinators, in what nesting, with what literal strings and glyphs.
83	
84	## Solver-entangled inputs — pure render vs. "stays ported"
85	
86	The renderer is a `Theory → text` function ONLY for the surface below. Some
87	displayed data is COMPUTED by the ported closure/solver; the clean crate
88	RENDERS it but is handed it pre-computed, and the computation stays ported.
89	
90	CLEANLY REIMPLEMENTABLE (pure render — R1–R4):
91	* term rendering; signature block; facts; rules; macros/predicates;
92	  formula & restriction & lemma-statement rendering; the guarded-formula
93	  comment TEXT (given the guarded formula); the `variants (modulo AC)` block
94	  TEXT (given the substitutions).
95	
96	STAYS PORTED (solver/closure output, NOT pure `Theory→text`):
97	* **Proof trees / proof methods / constraint-system pane** (`pretty_system.rs`,
98	  the `pp_proof`/`solve_goal_to_doc`/`pp_contradiction` paths in
99	  `pretty_theory.rs`). Only rendered under `--prove` or in the web UI, so they
100	  are OUTSIDE the no-prove echo the gate observes. Leave them ported.
101	* **The DATA feeding R2/R3's comments**: the AC-variant substitutions come from
102	  Maude (`tools/rule_variants`), the guarded-formula negation from the guarded
103	  transform, the closed signature (merged/sorted symbols) from theory closure.
104	  The clean crate consumes these; the transforms stay ported.
105	* **`--auto-sources` injection** (the 16 gate DIFFs): with `--auto-sources` the
106	  closer injects `AUTO_IN_TERM_*`/`AUTO_OUT_TERM_*` action facts and an
107	  `AUTO_typing [sources]` lemma into the CLOSED theory. The RS no-prove path
108	  does not perform this injection, so its echo lacks them vs the HS reference —
109	  a **closure/preprocessing** gap, upstream of the renderer, not a pretty-print
110	  bug. Exclude this family via ALLOWLIST while iterating (see
111	  `round1/exclude_auto_sources.txt`); it is not the pretty slice's to fix.
112	
113	## Method requirements (per PROTOCOL.md)
114	
115	* Derive every glyph, operator precedence, wrap column, spacing and ordering by
116	  experimenting against `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh`; take exact output strings
117	  from observed oracle output (compatibility content, never memory).
118	* Log every oracle query's purpose in `workspace/QUERIES.log` (one line each).
119	* Maintain `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`: the growing behavioral spec you infer
120	  (operator → glyph, precedence/parenthesization, wrap width, section order).
121	  A deliverable equal in weight to the code.
122	* Tests: per sub-target, a fixture (AST value as Rust constructors) + expected
123	  render snippet, asserted against your impl AND spot-checked against the
124	  oracle. Integration truth is the full-corpus gate.
125	* Dependencies: std only (plus the reused BSD Doc engine). You may NOT parse
126	  `.spthy` yourself; your input is the `ast_types.rs` model.
127

1	# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block; R2: rule blocks; R3: restriction/lemma formulas)
2	
3	Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
4	round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
5	"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
6	.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
7	workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).
8	
9	## Layout engine
10	
11	HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
12	observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
13	settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:
14	
15	* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
16	  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
17	  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
18	  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
19	* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
20	  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
21	  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
22	  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
23	* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
24	  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3

27	* **Deep-structure robustness (R4 blocker 3, BYTE-NEUTRAL).** A rendered Doc is
28	  a per-LINE linked chain (`NilAbove`/`TextBeside`/`Nest` interleaved with
29	  memoised `Lazy(Forced)` thunks) as deep as the theory has lines. A huge
30	  `variants (modulo AC)` block reaches ~10 000 lines (C8's
31	  `Terminal_Receives_Records_LocalAuth_C8` is 9 886 lines; BP_IBS_2/3/4 ~5–6k),
32	  so the naive recursive `reduceDoc`/`reduceVert`/`reduceHoriz` (build), the
33	  display walk (`lay`), and — the last one to surface — the compiler-generated
34	  recursive `Drop` of the nested `Rc<Doc>` all overflow a normal 8 MB stack
35	  (measured: overflow between ~1 500 and ~2 000 two-line groups). The wide FILL
36	  machinery is already lazy and does not overflow; only these eager `Above`/
37	  spine/drop walks do. All four are rewritten to iterate over a heap `Vec`
38	  (spine unrolled then folded; `lay` a loop; an explicit-stack `Drop` for
39	  `Doc`) — a pure evaluation-order change, so every rendered byte is identical
40	  (whole existing R1/R2/R3 suites unchanged) and the four deep witness files
41	  render completely and byte-match the oracle. Proof: a 12 000-group block
42	  renders on a 2 MB stack, and the round-2 curated set (now including the four
43	  deep files) passes on a production 8 MB stack, not the former 512 MB one.

198	* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
199	  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
200	  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
201	  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
202	  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
203	  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
204	  The application `)` is the plain BESIDE `<> ")"`, NOT a droppable fill item:
205	  when the last argument is a multi-line tuple whose `>` drops to its own line,
206	  the `)` STAYS JOINED to it as `>)` (probe:appdrop `someop(…, <…, macf(…)>)`
207	  → `…dd>)`; probe:appdrop2 nested → `…>))`; the round-2 blocker-2 corpus —
208	  ake/dh UM_three_pass etc. — has MAC applications with this exact shape). This
209	  is the crux of R4 blocker 2: the enclosing-delimiter drop is operator-
210	  specific — an AC-operator/pair `)`/`>` DROPS (fill item), an application `)`
211	  does NOT (beside). Changing `app_doc` to drop the `)` would break these
212	  witnesses; the fix is to leave it beside.
213	* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
214	  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
215	  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
216	  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
217	  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
218	  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
219	    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
220	  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
221	  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
222	    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
223	  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line
224	    (p_pw1 wfa/wfb/wfd), but drops to its own line at the column of `<`
225	    (no nest) when the last element is multi-line (p_pw1 wfc; mesh k2
226	    outer tuple) — the fill places items after a multi-line item below it.
227	  The earlier R1 law (`"<" <> fcat … <> ">"`, `<`/`>` always attached) was
228	  WRONG — falsified by mesh k2 / p_pw1; it agreed with the true law only on
229	  the shapes R1 observed (fits, and wfa/wfd-style wraps).
230	  Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
231	  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
232	  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).

238	  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
239	  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,

246	  `fcat ('(' : map (nest 1) (punctuate op elems) ++ [')'])`:

270	
271	Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
272	blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
273	parity). A rule BLOCK is:
274	
275	```

277	   <body>
278	<blank line>
279	  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
280	  <variants comment>              (col 2)

283	### Header
284	
285	* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
286	  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
287	* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, process="…",
288	  no_derivcheck, issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < process <
289	  no_derivcheck < issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST
290	  color/process/role declaration wins; external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
291	  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
292	  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` single-quoted.
293	  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
294	  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).
295	* **SAPIC `process` attribute** (R4 blocker-1 correction; probe:p_process,
296	  target:ct — the earlier "process is DROPPED" law was WRONG, from a corpus
297	  that had no `issapicrule` files in the round-2 set). It is present on every
298	  SAPIC-generated (`issapicrule`) rule, ABSENT otherwise, and renders
299	  `process="<snippet>"` — DOUBLE quotes (contrast `role='…'`), positioned
300	  between `color` and `no_derivcheck`. The `<snippet>` is the translated
301	  process step, carried VERBATIM as one unbreakable text token: `process="|"`
302	  (parallel), `process="!"` (replication), `process="0"` (null),
303	  `process="in(x.1);"`, `process="event Ev( x.1 );"`,
304	  `process="out(<x.1, 'lbl'>);"`, `process="lookup <'att', h.3> as a.1"`,
305	  `process="insert <$ca.2, 'proofOfID', $s.1, pk(skS.1)>,'yes';"`. Its interior
306	  spaces, commas, `<>`, `()`, `;` and SINGLE-quoted string constants are part
307	  of the value, NOT layout — the fill never breaks inside it (target:ct 100+
308	  witnesses; probe:p_process). ESCAPING of `"`/`\` is UNOBSERVABLE: process
309	  snippets quote their constants with `'…'`, so no `"`/`\`/control char ever
310	  reaches the attribute; rendered with no escaping (flagged — a corpus with a

398	## Formula rendering (R3)
399	
400	Curated byte targets: round3/targets/*.hs.txt (20 corpus files; 84
401	restriction + 139 lemma blocks, every block byte-verified by
402	tests/round3_formulas.rs round-trip parity).
403	
404	### Glyphs and atoms
405	
406	| construct | rendered | provenance |
407	|---|---|---|
408	| true / false | `⊤` / `⊥` | probe:q_w1 |
409	| conjunction / disjunction | `∧` / `∨` | probe:q_at1, q_p2 |
410	| implication / iff | `⇒` / `⇔` | probe:q_p2, q_r2 |
411	| negation | `¬(…)` — argument ALWAYS parenthesized | probe:q_p2 s8 |
412	| quantifiers | `∀` / `∃` | everywhere |
413	| action atom | `Fact( … ) @ tp` — spaces around `@` | probe:q_at1 |
414	| temporal/nat order | `t1 < t2` | probe:q_at1 |
415	| equality | `t1 = t2` | probe:q_at1 |
416	| subterm | `t1 ⊏ t2` (source `<<` and `⊏` both echo `⊏`) | probe:q_at1, target:NumberSubtermTests |
417	| last | `last(tp)` — NO interior spaces (unlike facts) | probe:q_at1 |
418	
419	Terms inside atoms render through the R1 term core unchanged (probe:q_l4 —
420	pair trailing-`", "` wrap, AC self-parens); action facts through the R2 fact
421	construction unchanged, `!` prefix included (target:NSLPK3 `!KU( ni ) @ #j`,
422	probe:q_l3).
423	
424	### Parenthesization
425	
426	* EVERY operand of a binary connective (∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔) is wrapped in `(…)`,
427	  whatever it is — atom, ⊤/⊥, ¬, quantifier, another connective
428	  (probe:q_p2 s1–s13; targets NSLPK3/Cronto/acc).
429	* `¬`'s argument is always wrapped (double wrap when ¬ is itself an
430	  operand: `(¬(x = 'd'))`).
431	* Quantifier BODIES and the top level are bare (probe:q_p2, q_w1).
432	* Chains render their source association faithfully — `a | b | c` parses
433	  left-nested upstream and echoes `((a) ∨ (b)) ∨ (c)`; no flattening,
434	  no re-association (probe:q_at1, probe:q_p2 s3–s6).
435	
436	### Layout
437	
438	* Binary connective = `sep [lhs-operand <+> glyph, rhs-operand]`: one line
439	  `(A) ∧ (B)` when it fits; otherwise the glyph stays on the lhs' LAST line
440	  and the rhs drops to the group origin (targets NSLPK3 types /
441	  Yubikey slightly_weaker_invariant / Cronto notSameRole deep left chain).
442	* Quantifier = `sep [glyph<>" "<>fsep binders<>".", nest 1 body]`:
443	  - binders are space-separated in source order with their R1 sigils and
444	    `.idx` suffixes (`∀ x.1 a.1 #i.1.` — probe:q_b1, target:acc lemmas);
445	  - the binder fill wraps aligned after `"∀ "` (origin+2 — probe:q_l2 bw1);
446	  - the `.` attaches to the last binder;
447	  - the body sits beside after one space, or drops to (quantifier origin+1)
448	    — the nest 1 (probe:q_l2 bw1/bw2; paren-nested `(((∃ #j.` → body at
449	    paren col + 2 confirms the same nest at depth).
450	* Relation atoms (`=` `<` `⊏`) = `sep [lhs-term <+> glyph, rhs-term]`: glyph
451	  attached to the lhs line, rhs drops to the atom origin (probe:q_l4 tw1–3).
452	* Action atom = `hsep [fact, "@", timepoint]`: `@ tp` NEVER drops alone —
453	  at overflow the FACT breaks internally (its `)` drops to the fact column,
454	  `@ tp` beside it: `) @ #i` — probe:q_l5 m63/m64 pins hsep over the
455	  sep-alternative, which would have kept `fact ) @` on one line).
456	* One-line fits follow the engine at 110/73 with the ribbon measured from
457	  the nest; the closing quote/parens attached after a formula count against
458	  its last line's fit (probe:q_l5 — 73-content + `"` broke).
459	
460	## Restriction blocks (R3)
461	
462	```
463	restriction Name:
464	  "formula"                (nest 2; quotes attach directly around the doc)
465	  // safety formula        (col 2 — iff the formula classifies safety)
466	<blank>
467	  /*                       (comment at nest 2)
468	  expanded formula:
469	  "formula"
470	  */
471	```
472	
473	* The `axiom` keyword echoes as `restriction` with the identical wrapper
474	  (probe:q_ax1, target:Cronto_EA).
475	* The expanded-formula comment content is byte-identical to the statement in
476	  every observation: predicate expansion happens UPSTREAM of both renderings
477	  (probe:q_pred1 — an MSR restriction through a predicate shows the
478	  expansion in BOTH spots; sapic corpus files agree). Rendered as the same
479	  formula twice.
480	* Safety classification (pinned by probes q_s1/q_s2 + q_w1 + 84-block corpus
481	  parity): a formula is safety iff its negation-normal form contains NO
482	  existential quantifier, msg-sort and temporal alike. Every ⇒-antecedent
483	  flips polarity; `¬∃` in a conclusion (∀ in NNF) keeps safety, `¬∃` in an
484	  antecedent (∃ in NNF) defeats it; a non-temporal `∃ y.` conclusion defeats
485	  it; top-level `¬(∃ …)` restrictions are safety (target:Yubikey).
486	* Sort-order of the comment lines: statement, safety line, blank, comment —
487	  the safety line sits BETWEEN the formula and the blank (probe:q_w1 r_eq).
488	
489	## Lemma blocks (R3)
490	
491	```
492	lemma Name [attr1, attr2]:
493	  all-traces|exists-trace "formula"    (sep at nest 2 — one line iff it fits)
494	/*                                     (comment at col 0)
495	guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:      (| all satisfying traces:)
496	"<guarded block>"                      (opaque input, verbatim lines)
497	*/
498	by sorry                               (| the embedded proof, verbatim)
499	```
500	
501	* Header: `lemma Name:`; with attributes `lemma Name [` + fill + `]:` — a
502	  SPACE before `[` (unlike rule attributes), items comma+space separated,
503	  fill-wrapped aligned after the `[` with `]:` attached to the last item;
504	  the first item stays beside the `[` even past the width (target:5G_AKA
505	  weakagreement_… at 120 display columns).
506	* Attributes render in SOURCE order, duplicates kept — NO canonicalization
507	  (probe:q_la1 `[hide_lemma=la1, use_induction, hide_lemma=la3, reuse]`).
508	  Spellings: `sources`, `reuse`, `use_induction`, `hide_lemma=<name>`,
509	  `heuristic=<value>` with the goal-ranking value verbatim (`S`, `{mytac}` —
510	  probes q_la2/q_la3; corpus `{sqn}` etc.).
511	* The trace-quantifier keyword is ALWAYS on the statement line, never the
512	  header line, even for `"⊤"` (probe:q_w1 l_top).
513	* Guarded comment at col 0, directly after the statement (no blank line):
514	  header `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:` for
515	  all-traces, `… all satisfying traces:` for exists-trace (probe:q_w1). The
516	  quoted content is OPAQUE pre-computed input (the ported guarded transform's
517	  rendering — note its style differs from the statement printer: n-ary ∧
518	  rows, lone `∧`/`⇒` lines, parenthesized ∃-body atoms; never produced by
519	  this crate).
520	* Failed conversion variant (probe:q_r1): header
521	  `conversion to guarded formula failed:` followed by the transform's error
522	  text with every line indented +2 (the raw error spelling, observed
523	  unindented in the fatal-restriction case, gains exactly 2 columns here).
524	* Tail: `by sorry` at col 0 when the source had no proof; a source-embedded
525	  proof re-renders after the `*/` instead (target:Yubikey `induction … qed`)
526	  — that proof text is PORTED-renderer output, carried verbatim as input.
527	* Restrictions outside the guarded fragment are a FATAL load error
528	  (probe:q_p1 raw), so the restriction renderer never sees them; lemmas
529	  outside it load fine and take the failed-conversion comment (probe:q_r1).
530

533	* PatMatch (`=x` pattern-match marker, sapic): cannot be forced through the
534	  MSR no-prove echo. Code renders `=` + term as a placeholder, flagged to
535	  this entry; must be pinned before any sapic-surface parity is claimed.
536	* SortHint::Suffix(s): the echo only ever shows sigil form (source `x:fresh`
537	  echoes `~x`, target:cav13), so suffix-tagged vars are rendered with the
538	  equivalent sigil.
539	* exp/mult element-level break choice: wide mult probes exceed the
540	  variant-computation budget (probe:t_mult timed out; 4-way
541	  probe:t_xorwide too). Union (probe:t_uniwide, round-3 target:alethea `)`
542	  drop) and Xor (probe:t_xorwide3, 3-way with long names: break after `⊕`
543	  attached to the preceding element, continuation after the `(`) pin the
544	  fill construction directly; Mult/NatPlus/exp-chains use the same
545	  construction by structural analogy — flagged, to be confirmed by the
546	  full-corpus gate at integration.
547	* reliable-channel builtin: requires a sapic top-level process (tool refuses
548	  otherwise — probe:b_reliable-channel raw); its signature contribution is
549	  unpinned in R1.
550	* Adapter contract notes: `NatLit`/`Number` carry DIGITS (renderer prefixes
551	  `%`); `NumberOne` is the DH `one`; `NatOne` is `%1`.
552	* Atom::LessMset (multiset smaller): no corpus witness and no reachable
553	  source spelling found (`(<)` absent from the whole corpus); rendered like
554	  `Less` as a flagged placeholder — must be pinned before any claim.
555	* Atom::Pred (predicate fact atoms): predicates are expanded upstream of the
556	  echo in BOTH the restriction statement and the expanded-formula comment
557	  (probe:q_pred1) and in sapic lemmas (corpus timepoints.spthy), so a Pred
558	  atom never reaches the no-prove renderer; rendered as a bare fact,
559	  flagged.
560	* Diff-mode surfaces (`--diff`): diffLemma items, lemma/restriction
561	  `left`/`right` attributes, `diff_reuse`. The parity corpus EXCLUDES all
562	  --diff files (RS cannot load them), so these never reach the gate; not
563	  modeled in the crate AST. A later round must pin them before any
564	  diff-file parity is claimed.
565	* Lemma modulo annotation (`lemma (modulo E) …`): never appears in any
566	  round-3 capture (grep across all 20); not modeled.
567	* Lemma attributes beyond the observed five (`output=…`, `Hint`, prover
568	  internals): no corpus/probe witness under the no-prove echo; not modeled.
569	* Fact annotations (`[+]`/`[-]`/`[no_precomp]`) on ACTION atoms inside
570	  formulas: never observed in any formula echo; the test parser rejects
571	  them loudly if they ever appear.
572	* ⇔ inside a safety-relevant restriction: no witness (⇔ restrictions are
573	  rare-to-absent in the corpus); the classifier treats each ⇔ side as
574	  occurring in both polarities (the NNF-consistent reading), flagged.
575	* Guarded-formula content and embedded proof scripts are PORTED-renderer
576	  output consumed verbatim as input — their interior layout is not a claim
577	  of this crate (only the comment frame and headers are).
578	* Restrictions whose formula falls outside the guarded fragment: fatal
579	  upstream error (probe:q_p1), unreachable for the renderer.

1	# Required public API (interoperability surface) — pretty-clean
2	
3	The clean crate must expose these entry points so the open side can wire it in
4	behind thin adapters that translate the live `tamarin_parser::ast` /
5	closed-theory values into the crate's own AST subset. Every function returns
6	text that is byte-identical to the oracle's no-prove theory echo for the
7	corresponding fragment. Names are indicative; the boundary that matters is
8	"one entry point per observable sub-target, each independently gate-checkable".
9	
10	```rust
11	// ── (top) whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end` (minus the wf
12	//    comment and the Generated-from stamp, which are appended by callers). ──
13	pub fn render_theory(thy: &Theory, sig: &Signature) -> String;
14	
15	// ── (a) signature block: builtins / functions / equations declarations ──
16	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &Signature) -> String;
17	
18	// ── (b) macros / predicates blocks ──
19	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[Macro]) -> String;        // "macros: …"
20	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[Predicate]) -> String; // "predicates: …"
21	
22	// ── (c) rule rendering: one multiset-rewrite rule, plus fact & TERM render ──
23	pub fn render_rule(rule: &Rule, variants: Option<&AcVariants>) -> String;
24	pub fn render_fact(fact: &Fact) -> String;               // `Foo( a, b )`
25	pub fn render_term(term: &Term) -> String;               // THE deep core
26	
27	// ── (d) restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering ──
28	pub fn render_restriction(r: &Restriction) -> String;
29	pub fn render_lemma(l: &Lemma, guarded: Option<&Guarded>) -> String;
30	pub fn render_formula(f: &Formula) -> String;            // ∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ @ ⊏ …
31	
32	// Layout primitive (BSD-provenance, NOT part of the GPL-erasure surface):
33	// the HughesPJ `Doc` engine + combinators. Reuse the graphdot cluster's
34	// clean-room engine (workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs, derived from
35	// sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6). Width = 110, ribbon = 73 for the theory echo.
36	```
37	
38	`Theory`, `Term`, `Fact`, `Formula`, `Atom`, `Rule`, `Lemma`, `Restriction`,
39	`Macro`, `Predicate`, `VarSpec`, `SortHint`, `BinOp`: see `ast_types.rs`.
40	
41	`Signature`, `AcVariants`, `Guarded` are pre-computed inputs supplied by the
42	ported closure/solver (SPEC.md "Solver-entangled inputs"); the clean crate
43	only RENDERS them, and their observable text is learned from the oracle.

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! Implemented sub-targets: R1 (term core + signature block), R2 (rule
8	//! blocks + macros surface), R3 (restriction / lemma / formula blocks).
9	//! Still stubs: R4 predicates block and the top-level theory assembly
10	//! (`theory::render`).
11	
12	pub mod ast;
13	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
14	
15	pub mod formula; // R3
16	pub mod lemma; // R3
17	pub mod macros; // R4
18	pub mod rule; // R2
19	pub mod signature; // R1
20	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
21	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
22	
23	pub use ast::*;
24	
25	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
26	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
27	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
28	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
29	    theory::render(thy, sig)
30	}
31	
32	/// R1: the signature section — header comment, blank line, then the
33	/// `builtins:` / `functions:` / `equations:` declarations. See
34	/// `signature::render`.
35	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
36	    signature::render(sig)

39	/// R1: one term, rendered at the echo's layout parameters. See `term::render`.
40	pub fn render_term(term: &ast::Term) -> String {
41	    term::render(term)

44	/// R2: one whole rule block — header, body, blank line, loop-breaker line,
45	/// and the AC-variants comment (`None` renders the trivial-variant
46	/// one-liner). See `rule::render`.
47	pub fn render_rule(r: &ast::Rule, variants: Option<&ast::AcVariants>) -> String {
48	    rule::render(r, variants)

5	//! lemmas), then `end`. The trailing wellformedness comment and the
6	//! `/* Generated from: … */` stamp are NOT emitted here — they are appended by
7	//! the caller (wf is a separate slice; the stamp is volatile) and the
8	//! acceptance gate strips both from each side. Section spacing/blank-line
9	//! discipline is observed from the oracle.

1	//! `macros:` block (rule-adjacent surface — appears in the echo between the
2	//! signature and the rules; target:issue777, probe:p_mac1) and the
3	//! `predicates:` block (R4, still unimplemented).
4	//!
5	//! ```text
6	//! macros: m1( x, y ) =  h(<x, y>),
7	//!         m2( ) =  h('c')
8	//! ```
9	//!
10	//! * block = `text "macros: " <> sep (punctuate ',' items)` — all-or-nothing:
11	//!   one line when everything fits, otherwise EVERY macro on its own line
12	//!   aligned after `macros: ` (probe:p_mac1 — `m2( )` gets its own line even
13	//!   though it would fit beside `m1`);
14	//! * item = `hsep [name( <+> fsep params <+> ")", "= ", body]` — the head is
15	//!   fact-style (`m2( )` when nullary; params fill-wrap aligned after the
16	//!   paren) but its `)` stays ATTACHED to the last param line, and the `= `
17	//!   token plus hsep spacing yields the observed `) =  body` (two spaces
18	//!   after `=`); the body always sits beside, wrapping internally.
19	
20	use crate::ast::{Macro, Predicate};
21	use crate::doc::{
22	    beside_op, beside_space, char, fsep, hsep, punctuate, render_with, sep,
23	    text, Doc,
24	};
25	use crate::term::{self, RIBBON, WIDTH};
26	
27	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[Macro]) -> String {
28	    render_with(WIDTH, RIBBON, &macros_doc(macros))
29	}
30	
31	fn macros_doc(macros: &[Macro]) -> Doc {
32	    beside_op(
33	        text("macros: "),
34	        sep(punctuate(
35	            &char(','),
36	            macros.iter().map(macro_doc).collect(),
37	        )),
38	    )
39	}
40	
41	fn macro_doc(m: &Macro) -> Doc {
42	    let head = beside_space(
43	        beside_space(
44	            text(&format!("{}(", m.name)),
45	            fsep(punctuate(
46	                &char(','),
47	                m.params.iter().map(term::doc).collect(),
48	            )),
49	        ),
50	        char(')'),
51	    );
52	    hsep(vec![head, text("= "), term::doc(&m.body)])
53	}
54	
55	pub fn render_predicates(_preds: &[Predicate]) -> String {
56	    unimplemented!("R4: predicates block")
57	}

13	/// Multiset-rewriting term. Rendered by `term::render`.
14	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]

17	    /// Public string constant; renders quoted: `'name'`.
18	    PubLit(String),
19	    /// Fresh name constant; renders `~'name'`.
20	    FreshLit(String),

28	    NatOne,

30	    DhNeutral,
31	    /// Function application `f(a, b, …)`; arity 0 renders bare (`f`).
32	    App(String, Vec<Term>),
33	    /// Named binary algebra operator; `"exp"` renders infix `a^b`, anything
34	    /// else falls back to application form.
35	    AlgApp(String, Box<Term>, Box<Term>),

37	    /// tuple, a Pair in any other position keeps its own delimiters.

50	/// render `(a<op>b<op>c)` — self-parenthesized, flattened, no spaces.

6	# prints the EXTRACTED THEORY-ECHO BLOCK — the exact `theory <name> begin …
7	# end` text your reimplementation must reproduce byte-for-byte.

17	# after `end` (the summary-of-summaries).  Interior comments — rule AC-variant
18	# blocks and `guarded formula characterizing …` — are KEPT; they are your
19	# output.  This is identical to the extraction the acceptance gate applies to
20	# both sides, so `diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(your_binary f | extract)` == the
21	# gate's verdict.

String: /// `predicate: <fact><=>formula` per predicate. The whole group renders with
/// a blank line between successive predicates — the frame treats the group as
/// one item (BEHAVIOR.md "Predicates block").
pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[Predicate]) -> String {
    render_with(WIDTH, RIBBON, &predicates_doc(preds))
}

pub(crate) fn predicates_doc(preds: &[Predicate]) -> Doc {
    let mut rows: Vec<Doc> = Vec::new();
    for (i, p) in preds.iter().enumerate() {
        if i > 0 {
            // Blank line between predicates. `text("")` is a present-but-empty
            // line; `empty()` would be dropped by `vcat`'s `$$` (no gap).
            rows.push(text(""));
        }
        rows.push(predicate_doc(p));
    }
    vcat(rows)
}

/// `predicate: Name( args )<=>formula` — `predicate: ` prefix, the R2 fact
/// head built from the predicate's name + parameter variables, the bare `<=>`
/// glyph (no surrounding spaces), then the R3 formula body.
fn predicate_doc(p: &Predicate) -> Doc {
    let fact = Fact {
        persistent: false,
        name: p.name.clone(),
        args: p.params.iter().map(|v| Term::Var(v.clone())).collect(),
        annotations: vec![],
    };
    let head = beside_op(
        beside_op(text("predicate: "), fact_doc(&fact)),
        text("<=>"),
    );
    beside_op(head, formula::doc(&p.body))
}

## Theory frame (R5 GAP-3)

Whole-echo assembly `theory <name> begin … end`. Curated byte targets: the
round1-3 `targets/*.hs.txt` whole captures; 29 diverse files
(tests/round5_theory.rs `whole_echo_frame_parity`) byte-verified end-to-end
(builtins variety, macros, rules ± variants, loop breakers, SAPIC process
attrs, restrictions ± safety ± expanded, lemmas all/exists ± attrs ± embedded
proof, predicates, heuristic, tactic, section comments).

* **Header / footer.** Always `theory NAME` then a blank, `begin`, a blank
  (all 69 surveyed captures). No `configuration` string observed. Close: the
  extracted echo ALWAYS ends with exactly THREE blank lines then `end` (all 44
  surveyed captures). Those blanks are the residue the gate extraction leaves
  after dropping the two trailing comment blocks that are OUT of this crate's
  span — the wellformedness report and the `Generated from:` stamp — each a
  single blank-separated slot, plus the blank before `end` (RAW tail of
  target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions verified: `by sorry` · blank · wf-line ·
  blank · Generated-block · blank · `end`).
* **Signature first.** The signature block (`// Function signature …` + blank +
  `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:`) is ALWAYS the first thing after
  `begin`, even ahead of `tactic:` blocks (target:5G_AKA) and `heuristic:`
  (target:contract). It is rendered from the `Signature`, never a theory item.
* **Item order + spacing.** All other blocks are theory items in SOURCE order;
  the frame stacks them with ONE blank line between successive items. Item
  types observed: `macros:`, `predicate:` (grouped), rules, restrictions,
  lemmas, `heuristic:`, `tactic:`, `section{* … *}` formal comments, and the
  theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: … */` note.
  Assembly = `join(["theory NAME", "begin", sig, items…], "\n\n") + "\n\n\n\nend"`.
* **`heuristic:` line.** A single `heuristic: <value>` item (targets contract,
  ct, running-example, accountability — value e.g. `p`, `o "oracle" …`).
* **`predicate:` block.** One `predicate: <fact><=><formula>` per predicate; a
  contiguous run renders as one item, blank-line separated. NO spaces around
  `<=>`; the head is the R2 fact (`True( x )`), the body the R3 formula. The
  body wraps at ABSOLUTE MARGIN 0 (column-1 nest), INDEPENDENT of the header
  width (target:dmn-basic — `Sender_duplicate` and `Mixer_duplicate` bodies
  both wrap at col 1 despite different name lengths, and a 66-column body row
  fits ribbon 73 measured from col 1, not from the `<=>` column). A
  `<>`-beside composition would instead indent the body under `<=>` — the
  sanctioned HughesPJ `display` threads the current line width into each
  `NilAbove` continuation (`lay2 k (NilAbove p) = nl `txt` lay k p`;
  pretty-1.1.3.6 confirmed) — so the renderer splices the header textually onto
  the margin-0 formula's first line instead of `<>`-ing it.
* **Opaque / verbatim items.** `tactic:` (the whole contiguous region — sub-
  tactics `presort:`/`prio:` carry NO blank-line separators, so the region is
  one block; target:5G_AKA/alethea), `section{* … *}` formal comments
  (target:Joux/Yubikey/contract), and top-level `/* … */` comments (looping-
  facts note) are carried through the frame VERBATIM — their interior layout is
  outside this crate's erasure surface, like guarded/proof text.
